five_questions.problem: Fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs) with parameter-efficient methods like Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) remains structurally uncertain due to poorly understood layer-specific roles of internal representations.
five_questions.hard: The problem is hard because the layer-specific roles of internal representations are poorly understood, making it difficult to optimize layer selection for efficient fine-tuning.
five_questions.tried: Previous approaches included full 36-layer adaptation (79.32% performance) and random 13-layer selection (75.56% performance), both of which were outperformed by the new method.
five_questions.outcomes: The RDP LoRA method achieved superior performance on MMLU-Math (81.67%) using only 13 RDP-selected layers, outperforming full 36-layer adaptation (79.32%) and random 13-layer selection (75.56%).
five_questions.next: Further research is needed to explore the limitations of the RDP LoRA method, specifically regarding its assumption that the geometric trajectory of hidden states is a reliable indicator of layer importance.
